correction_note.correction: No ghost bet existed for CLE@NYK Game 1. Prior wording implying a ghost/team-total win on this matchup was incorrect and has been removed from the position audit.
correction_note.source_of_truth: User position screenshots show only settled losses: Harden 7+ assists, Josh Hart 9+ rebounds, No NYK by over 8.5, and No NYK by over 9.5.

spread.settled_result: NYK covered most pregame spreads; OT margin distortion created spread failure for CLE/backdoor-style No positions.

total.note: Total landed near closing band; OT created upper-tail total pressure. No active ghost bet or logged official team-total position for this matchup.

NYK.position_status: No user bet / no ghost bet logged

CLE.position_status: No user bet / no ghost bet logged

core_game_story.primary_read: Cleveland controlled the middle of the game and led by 22 in the fourth, but New York flipped the game through pressure, Brunson isolation hunting, Cleveland turnover leakage, offensive rebounding/extra possessions, and overtime margin expansion.
core_game_story.critical_state: CLE led 93-71 with 7:52 left in Q4 before NYK finished regulation plus OT on a 44-11 run.
core_game_story.model_relevance: The model underweighted collapse risk once CLE had a large fourth-quarter lead. OT margin distortion and Brunson switch-hunt pressure must be elevated in future playoff LIVE-FLOW states.

team_efficiency_summary.diagnostic: Shot quality was equal at 0.56, but NYK won through two-point pressure, rim conversion, free throws, rebounding volume, and late turnover conversion rather than pure shot-quality dominance.

Josh Hart.prop_result: Missed 9+ ladder despite viable chance volume; minutes capped at 30.5 and rebound tree condensed toward Towns/Robinson/Mobley zones.

Mitchell Robinson.diagnostic: Elite per-minute rebound capture; Robinson minutes remain high-leverage for NYK rebound environment and Hart suppression.

box_out_notes.NYK: OG Anunoby led with 5 box outs; Towns had 2; Hart, Brunson, Robinson, McBride each had 1. NYK generated more useful box-out-to-team-rebound conversion than raw box-out totals suggest.
box_out_notes.CLE: Mobley had 2 box outs, Allen 1, Merrill 1. CLE's box-out profile did not prevent NYK from winning total rebounds 58-49 and offensive rebounds 17-15.

rebounding_profile.model_patch: Hart rebound overs require a Robinson-minute displacement gate. If Robinson projects 14+ minutes with elite contested capture, reduce Hart rebound median by 0.8-1.4 unless Hart minutes project 38+.

turnover_profile.lineup_failure: CLE lineups containing Mitchell/Strus/Merrill/Mobley/Harden and Mitchell/Allen/Strus/Mobley/Harden showed elevated live-ball leakage in short samples. Harden late-game ball security and defensive targeting became a dual liability.
turnover_profile.model_patch: Add PLAYOFF_PRESSURE_LIVEBALL_DECAY: when a team leads by 15+ in Q4 but has 15+ turnovers and opponent has elite pressure/scoring guard, reduce lead safety and spread-cover probability by 6-10 points.

foul_profile.interpretation: Foul environment was high but not one-sided enough to explain the full comeback. The bigger betting impact came from late rim pressure and OT margin extension.

q1.read: NYK established defensive pressure early while CLE struggled from field. Early Knicks lead hinted at pressure upside but not full-game stability.

q2.read: Cleveland stabilized through Mitchell creation and three-point volume. Knicks offense showed rust and spacing issues.

q3.read: CLE appeared to break the game open. Model should have flagged potential CLE cover state but also monitored turnover accumulation and Harden defensive exposure.

q4.read: Game flipped. Brunson attack pressure and NYK defense overwhelmed CLE. This is the central model failure phase: large-lead safety was overestimated.

ot.read: OT created margin distortion. Pre-OT/live spread positions were punished by momentum cascade and Cleveland offensive collapse.

official_user_positions.position: No - New York wins by over 9.5
official_user_positions.reason: Final NYK +11 after OT margin distortion

official_user_positions.position: No - New York wins by over 8.5
official_user_positions.reason: Final NYK +11 after OT margin distortion
Hart had 15 chances but only 30.5 minutes and rebound capture concentrated through Towns/Robinson
Cleveland offense shifted toward Mitchell scoring/late breakdown; Harden assist flow did not materialize

betting_position_audit.ghost_bet_status: NONE. No ghost bet was placed or logged for CLE@NYK Game 1.
betting_position_audit.root_cause: The card clustered around CLE staying inside margin plus Hart/Harden connective stats. All three depended on Cleveland offensive stability and non-collapse conditions, creating correlated downside exposure.
betting_position_audit.risk_patch: LIVEFLOW_RISK_CLUSTER_LOCK must treat spread protection + opponent rebound/assist props as correlated when they depend on the same pace/lead state. Max two correlated open exposures.

patch_queue.trigger: Team leads by 15+ in Q4 while carrying 15+ turnovers or negative live-ball trend
patch_queue.effect: Lower cover probability and raise comeback/OT tail probability

patch_queue.trigger: Brunson vs weak lateral guard/older ball-handler defender in Q4/OT
patch_queue.effect: Increase NYK late rim pressure, free throw rate, and opponent foul/turnover risk

patch_queue.trigger: Mitchell Robinson 14+ projected minutes with high contested rebound capture
patch_queue.effect: Reduce Hart/wing rebound over probability unless minutes and long-miss profile compensate

patch_queue.trigger: Live spread ladder positions inside 10 points during late tied/comeback states
patch_queue.effect: Reduce stake or avoid unless edge exceeds 8% and market price gives cushion

CLE: Must protect ball late; 21 turnovers is disqualifying for spread trust.
Mitchell remains primary scoring pressure point, but Harden must be treated as volatility source defensively and as a turnover risk.
Mobley rebound profile is stable and should remain a viable rebound anchor.
NYK: Brunson late-game pressure is now elevated to primary series driver.
Towns plus Robinson gives NYK a more condensed and physical rebound tree than Hart ladder pricing implied.
NYK team total market result was over, but no active user/ghost position existed. Do not count it in betting ledger.
